title: AGENTS.md
text: BRIER (Bregman-divergence-based regularized integrative estimator regression) R package transfer-learning genetic risk prediction. Target-cohort prediction improved borrowing information external cohorts, pretrained external coefficients, summary-level association statistics, high-dimensional regularization. Three top-level fitting functions cover three external-data regimes: BRIERfull (raw external), BRIERi (pretrained coefficients individual-level target), BRIERs (pretrained coefficients summary-level target). package roughly 78% R 21% C++. Heavy numerical work (coordinate descent solvers, LD assembly) lives C++ via Rcpp + RcppArmadillo; high-level orchestration, model selection, plotting live R. License: GPL-3. Maintainer: Ruiwen Zhou (zrayw@umich.edu).

title: Information Criteria for Hyper-parameter Tuning
text: implement several information-criterion–based model selection procedures BRIERi.selection, including Bayesian information criterion (BIC), Mallows’ \(C_p\), generalized cross-validation (GCV), select tuning parameter \(\eta\) \(\lambda\). Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC). BIC defined \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:BIC} \mathrm{BIC}(\eta,\lambda) =   \frac{1}{n} \left\{   \mathrm{Dev}\!\left(y,\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\eta,\lambda}\right)   +     \log(n)\,\mathrm{df}(\eta,\lambda)   \right\}, \end{equation}\] \(\mathrm{Dev}(y,\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\eta,\lambda})\) denotes deviance evaluated fitted model tuning parameters \((\eta,\lambda)\), \(\mathrm{df}(\eta,\lambda)\) corresponding effective degrees freedom. Mallows’ \(C_p\). linear models known error variance \(\sigma^2\), Mallows’ \(C_p\) defined \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:Cp} C_p(\widehat{\mathbf{\mu}}) =   \frac{1}{n} \left\| \mathbf{y} - \widehat{\mathbf{\mu}} \right\|^2 +   \frac{2\sigma^2}{n}\, \mathrm{df}, \end{equation}\] \(\widehat{\mathbf{\mu}}\) denotes vector predicted means \(\mathrm{df}\) associated degrees freedom. Generalized Cross-Validation (GCV). Generalized cross-validation provides computationally efficient approximation leave-one-cross-validation given \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:GCV} \mathrm{GCV}(\lambda) =   \frac{\mathrm{Dev}(y,\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\lambda})} {n \left\{ 1 - \mathrm{df}(\lambda)/n \right\}^2}. \end{equation}\]

title: Information Criteria for Hyper-parameter Tuning
text: implement several model selection procedures BRIERs.selection summary-level data, including pseudo-validation, Bregman-divergence–based Mallows’ \(C_p\) (\(BRC_p\)), generalized information criteria (GIC), select tuning parameters \(\eta\) \(\lambda\). Pseudo-validation. Originally proposed LASSOsum , pseudo-validation selects tuning parameter \(\lambda\) maximizing objective \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:pseudoval} f(\lambda) =   \frac{\mathbf{\beta}_{\lambda}^{\top}\,\widehat{\mathbf{r}}} {\sqrt{\mathbf{\beta}_{\lambda}^{\top}\,\widetilde{\mathbf{X}}_{0}^{\top}   \widetilde{\mathbf{X}}_{0}\,\mathbf{\beta}_{\lambda}}}, \end{equation}\] \(\widetilde{\mathbf{X}}_{0}\) standardized genotype matrix external reference panel \(\widehat{\mathbf{r}}\) shrinkage-adjusted estimate SNP–phenotype correlation vector. Specifically, element \(\widehat{\mathbf{r}}\) defined \[   \widehat{r}_j   =     r_j\left(1-\mathrm{fdr}_j\right),   \] \(\mathrm{fdr}_j\) denotes local false discovery rate SNP \(j\). objective approximates Spearman correlation predicted genetic score \(\widetilde{\mathbf{X}}_{0}\mathbf{\beta}_{\lambda}\) unobserved true phenotype. Bregman-divergence based \(C_p\). summary-level settings, adopt Bregman-divergence based extension Mallows’ \(C_p\), referred BRC\(_p\). criterion defined \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:BRCp} \widehat{R}_{\mathrm{BRC}_p} \!\left(\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\eta,\lambda}\right) =   D_{\mathcal{E}_{\widetilde{\mathbf{\Sigma}}}} \!\left(   \widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\eta,\lambda},   \widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}   \right) +   \frac{|\mathcal{S}_{\eta,\lambda}|}{n(1+\eta)} \, D_{\mathcal{E}_{\widetilde{\mathbf{\Sigma}}}} \!\left(   \widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\eta,\lambda},   \widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}   \right), \end{equation}\] \(D_{\mathcal{E}_{\widetilde{\mathbf{\Sigma}}}}(\cdot,\cdot)\) denotes Bregman divergence induced quadratic loss respect linkage disequilibrium (LD) matrix \(\widetilde{\mathbf{\Sigma}}\), \(\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}\) pilot reference coefficient estimate, \(|\mathcal{S}_{\eta,\lambda}|\) size active set associated tuning parameters \((\eta,\lambda)\). factor \(1+\eta\) accounts effective sample-size inflation induced external model integration. Generalized Information Criterion (GIC). implement generalized information criterion summary-level models, defined \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:GIC_s} \mathrm{GIC}(\eta,\lambda) =   \frac{1}{n} \left\{   G\!\left(\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\eta,\lambda}\right)   +     a_n\,\mathrm{df}(\eta,\lambda)   \right\}, \end{equation}\] \(G(\cdot)\) denotes goodness--fit measure expressed terms summary statistics, \(\mathrm{df}(\eta,\lambda)\) effective degrees freedom defined previous section, \(a_n\) penalty term controlling model complexity. Throughout work, set \(a_n = \log(n)\), corresponding Bayesian information criterion (BIC). BRIERs, goodness--fit term \(G(\cdot)\) evaluated using Bregman divergence associated quadratic loss induced linkage disequilibrium matrix.

title: Information Criteria for Hyper-parameter Tuning
text: Accurate estimation degrees freedom (DoF) essential information-criterion–based model selection procedures. BRIERi BRIERs can formulated penalized regression problems respect external-model–weighted response, denoted \(\widetilde{\mathbf{Y}}\) formulation, effective degrees freedom fitted model can explicitly expressed \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:df_weighted} \mathrm{df} =   \frac{\mathrm{df}(\widetilde{\mathbf{Y}})}{1+\eta}, \end{equation}\] \(\mathrm{df}(\widetilde{\mathbf{Y}})\) denotes degrees freedom associated weighted response, \(\eta\) integration weight. BRIER, implement two approaches estimate degrees freedom penalized models. first approach adopts simple computationally efficient estimator uses number active predictors, .e., number nonzero regression coefficients fitted model, estimate degrees freedom. estimator shown consistent LASSO-type problems suitable regularity conditions . second approach implements general degrees--freedom estimator based generalized information criterion (GIC) . Specifically, degrees freedom defined \[\begin{equation} \label{eq:df_gic} \mathrm{df}_{\lambda} \;=\; \mathrm{tr} \left\{   \left[     \nabla^{2}_{\mathbf{\beta}}     Q_{\eta, \lambda}\!\left(\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\lambda}\right)     \right]^{-1}   \nabla^{2}_{\mathbf{\beta}}   \ell\!\left(\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\lambda}\right)   \right\}, \end{equation}\] \[   Q_{\eta, \lambda}(\mathbf{\beta})   =     Q_{\eta}(\mathbf{\beta})   +     p_{\lambda}(\mathbf{\beta})   \] denotes penalized objective function penalty function \(p_{\lambda}(\mathbf{\beta})\) \(\widehat{\mathbf{\beta}}_{\lambda}\) estimated coefficient vector tuning parameter \(\lambda\).

title: Model Details
text: BRIER.FULL procedure aims minimize following objective function: \[ Q_{\mathbf{\eta}}(\mathbf{\beta}) = -\, \ell\!\left(\mathbf{\beta}\mid \mathbf{X}, \mathbf{Y}\right) - \sum_{m=1}^{M} \eta_m\, \ell\!\left(\mathbf{\beta}\mid \check{\mathbf{X}}_m, \check{\mathbf{Y}}_m\right). \] integration weight \(\eta_m \ge 0\) controls extent information transferred \(m\)-th external cohort target data. , \(\ell(\cdot)\) denotes log-likelihood function. generalized linear models Cox proportional hazards model, corresponding log-likelihood functions specified .

title: Model Details
text: Assume conditional distribution \(Y_i\) given \(\mathbf{X}_i\) belongs canonical exponential family, conditional density \[ f(Y_i; \theta_i, \phi) \propto \exp\left( \frac{Y_i \theta_i - b(\theta_i)}{(\phi)} \right), \] \((\cdot)\) \(b(\cdot)\) pre-specified functions corresponding assumed outcome distribution, \(\phi\) nuisance parameter related variance, \(\theta_i\) canonical parameter. mean variance satisfy \[ \mu_i = \mathbb{E}(Y_i \mid \theta_i) = b'(\theta_i), \] \[ \mathrm{Var}(Y_i \mid \theta_i) = (\phi)\, b''(\theta_i). \] mean \(\mu_i\) related linear predictor link function: \[ g(\mu_i) = \eta_i = \mathbf{x}_i^\top \mathbf{\beta}. \] log-likelihood function given \[ \ell(\mathbf{\beta}) = \sum_{=1}^n \log f\!\left(y_i \mid \theta_i(\mathbf{\beta}), \phi\right) = \sum_{=1}^n \left[ \frac{y_i \theta_i(\mathbf{\beta}) - b\!\left(\theta_i(\mathbf{\beta})\right)}{(\phi)} + c(y_i,\phi) \right]. \] (#tab:glm-family) Exponential-family distributions implemented BRIER, including cumulant functions \(b(\theta)\) canonical link functions \(g(\mu)\).

title: Model Details
text: Cox proportional hazard model, negative log-partial likelihood admits KL-divergence interpretation sequence conditional failure-time distributions. Let \(t_{(1)} < \cdots < t_{(K)}\) denote ordered distinct event times, let \(\mathcal{R}_k\) denote risk set immediately prior \(t_{(k)}\). subject \(i_k\) experiences event \(t_{(k)}\), working Cox model induces conditional probability \[ f^{(\mathbf{\beta})}(i_k \mid \mathcal{R}_k) = \frac{\exp(\mathbf{x}_{i_k}^{\top}\mathbf{\beta})} {\sum_{j\\mathcal{R}_k}\exp(\mathbf{x}_j^{\top}\mathbf{\beta})}. \] log-partial likelihood working Cox model \[ \ell_p(\mathbf{\beta}) = \sum_{k=1}^{K} \log f^{(\mathbf{\beta})}(i_k \mid \mathcal{R}_k) = \sum_{k=1}^{K} \left[ \mathbf{x}_{i_k}^{\top}\mathbf{\beta} - \log \left( \sum_{j\\mathcal{R}_k} \exp(\mathbf{x}_j^{\top}\mathbf{\beta}) \right) \right]. \]

title: Model Details
text: BRIER.procedure minimizes following objective: \[ Q_{\mathbf{\eta}}(\mathbf{\beta}) = -\, \ell\!\left(\mathbf{\beta}\mid \mathbf{X}, \mathbf{Y}\right) + \sum_{m=1}^{M} \eta_m\, \operatorname{D_{KL}}\!\left(\check{f}_m \,\|\, f\right). \] integration weight \(\eta_m \ge 0\) controls extent information transfer \(m\)-th external cohort target data. , \(\ell\!\left(\mathbf{\beta}\mid \mathbf{X}, \mathbf{Y}\right)\) log-likelihood evaluated target individual-level data, \(\operatorname{D_{KL}}\!\left(\check{f}_m \,\|\, f\right)\) Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence (???), measuring discrepancy density \(\check{f}_m\) induced external model \(\check{\mathbf{\beta}}_m\) density \(f\) induced target working model \(\mathbf{\beta}\). generalized linear models Cox proportional hazards model, corresponding KL divergences specified .

title: Model Details
text: summary-level local data available, individual-level quantities \(\{\mathbf{X}, \mathbf{Y}\}\) accessed. Instead, assume availability summary-level proxy statistics \[ \mathbf{r} = \frac{1}{n}\mathbf{X}^{\top}\mathbf{Y}, \qquad \mathbf{R} = \frac{1}{n}\mathbf{X}^{\top}\mathbf{X}, \] \(\mathbf{r}\) denotes score vector \(\mathbf{R}\) denotes LD (correlation) matrix estimated local data. setting, BRIERs extends ensemble framework BRIERi estimating ensemble weights using least-squares proxy objective depends \(\mathbf{r}\) \(\mathbf{R}\). Let \[ \mathbf{B} = \left( \check{\mathbf{\beta}}_{1}, \ldots, \check{\mathbf{\beta}}_{K} \right) \] denote matrix collecting external coefficient vectors, let \(\mathbf{w}\) denote ensemble weights. Consider proxy quadratic loss \[ \frac{1}{n}\big\|\mathbf{Y} - \mathbf{X}\mathbf{B}\mathbf{w}\big\|_2^2. \] Expanding expression using \(\mathbf{r}\) \(\mathbf{R}\) yields \[ \frac{1}{n}\|\mathbf{Y} - \mathbf{X}\mathbf{B}\mathbf{w}\|_2^2 = \text{const} - 2\,\mathbf{w}^{\top}\mathbf{B}^{\top}\mathbf{r} + \mathbf{w}^{\top}\mathbf{B}^{\top}\mathbf{R}\mathbf{B}\mathbf{w}. \] Setting derivative zero gives closed-form estimator ensemble weights: \[ \widehat{\mathbf{w}} = \big(\mathbf{B}^{\top}\mathbf{R}\mathbf{B}\big)^{-1} \mathbf{B}^{\top}\mathbf{r}. \] summary-level composite external model obtained \[ \check{\mathbf{\beta}} = \sum_{k=1}^{K} \widehat{w}_k\,\check{\mathbf{\beta}}_{k}. \] aggregated coefficient vector can plugged directly BRIER.S objective, enabling information borrowing multiple external models without requiring individual-level local data independent validation set. BRIERs therefore provides computationally efficient privacy-preserving approach multi-study integration summary-level constraints. Since cis-eQTL effects often shared across biologically related tissues, leveraging external models trained non-target tissues can substantially improve predictive performance target tissue, particularly tissues limited sample size. Naively integrating external model integration weight, however, leads hyperparameter space grows exponentially number external sources, making joint tuning computationally prohibitive. address , BRIER provides three strategies integrating multiple external models, controlled multi.method argument: "ind": external model assigned independent integration weight \(\eta_m\). flexible option, hyperparameter space grows multiplicatively number external sources, computationally practical number external models small (typically \(M \leq 3\)). "pca": external coefficient matrix reduced small number leading principal components, top components retained integration. collapses multidimensional penalty space low-dimensional one reduces tuning cost. code: library(BRIER)  data("Data_BRIERs") data <- Data_BRIERs  ## Shared target-cohort preprocessing X <- data$target$train$X summary <- data$target$train$sumstats summary$corr <- p2cor(summary$pval, summary$n, sign = sign(summary$stats)) XtX.list <- calLD(X, summary)  X_val <- standardize_X(data$target$validation$X)[[1]] y_val <- standardize_X(as.matrix(data$target$validation$y))[[1]]  eta.list <- c(0, exp(seq(log(0.1), log(10), length.out = 10)))  ## Fit, tune, and visualize one external model at a time for (m in 1:3) {   fit <- BRIERs(     sumstats = summary,     XtX = XtX.list$XtX,     beta.external = data$beta.external[, m],     eta.list = eta.list   )   fit <- BRIERs.selection(     fit, criteria = "gaussian.mspe",     X.val = X_val, y.val = y_val   )   out <- plot.eta(     fit,     X = data$target$testing$X,     covar.data = data$target$testing$y,     criteria = "gaussian.mspe",     standardize.data = TRUE,     bootstrap = TRUE   )   print(out$plot) }

text: function preprocessI() prepares genotype-based inputs BRIERi() individual-level genotype data available target cohort one external models available SNP-level coefficients. argument target.info takes data.frame describing SNPs target genotype matrix, row corresponds one column target predictor matrix. Users must specify column mapping target.info.cols. argument external.ss takes optional data.frame containing external model coefficients. external.ss provided, users must specify SNP-annotation column mapping external.ss.cols coefficient columns external.coef.cols. preprocessI() first performs quality control harmonization separately target SNP information external model coefficients. Specifically, : standardizes SNP annotation columns CHR, BP, REF, ALT, corresponding chromosome, base-pair position, reference allele, alternative allele; normalizes chromosome labels common format, including 1–22, X, Y, MT, XY; removes SNPs invalid unrecognized chromosome labels; removes multi-allelic sites, defined duplicated CHR:BP entries within input; optionally removes strand-ambiguous SNPs, including /T C/G variants, using drop.ambiguous argument. preprocessI() also aligns external SNP coefficients target cohort. Specifically, : SNP identifiers standardized using target-cohort allele orientation CHR:BP:REF:ALT. External SNPs matched target SNPs chromosome base-pair position (CHR:BP). external REF ALT alleles match target-cohort orientation, external coefficient retained unchanged. external REF ALT alleles reversed relative target-cohort orientation, external coefficient sign flipped. external alleles conflict target alleles, external coefficient set zero (conflict means alleles disagree beyond simple flip, e.g., /G vs /T position, usually indicating different variant). SNPs present external models absent target cohort removed. code: library(BRIER)  data("Data_preprocessI") dat <- Data_preprocessI names(dat) # [1] "target.info"    "external.coef1" "external.coef2" external.merged <- mergeExternals(list(dat$external.coef1, dat$external.coef2)) # Input SNPs per model (after CHR validation): external.list[[1]]=10000, external.list[[2]]=163140 # Union SNP set: 163140 variants across 2 model(s). #   model1 (-> coef1): matched=10000, flipped=0, conflict=0, zero-padded=153140 #   model2 (-> coef2): matched=161641, flipped=1499, conflict=0, zero-padded=0 processed <- preprocessI(    target.info = dat$target.info,    target.info.cols = c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "A2", alt = "A1"),   external.ss = external.merged,    external.ss.cols = c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "REF", alt = "ALT"),    external.coef.cols = c("coef1", "coef2") ) # Input SNPs: target.info=10000, external.ss=163140 # External: 9000 matched, 1000 flipped, 0 conflict, 0 zero-padded. # Final analysis set: 10000 SNPs.  names(processed) # [1] "target.info"         "target.info.keep"    "external.ss"         # [4] "external.coef.names" "summary" X <- X[, processed$target.info.keep] # BRIERi requires an intercept slot as the first row of beta.external; BRIERs does not. external.mat <- as.matrix(   processed$external.ss[, grep("^coef", colnames(processed$external.ss)), drop = FALSE] ) external.mat <- rbind(0, external.mat)  fit <- BRIERi(   X, y, family = "gaussian",    beta.external = external.mat,    multi.method = "stacking" )

LD.input <- dat$target.ld.mat[processed$target.ld.keep, processed$target.ld.keep] external.mat <- as.matrix(   processed$external.ss[, grep("^coef", colnames(processed$external.ss)), drop = FALSE] ) fit <- BRIERs(   sumstats = processed$target.ss, XtX = LD.input,   family = "gaussian",   beta.external = external.mat,    multi.method = "stacking" ) ldsc_input <- dat$target.ss ldsc_input$Z <- ldsc_input$STAT  ldsc_input$N <- ldsc_input$NMISS h2_res <- ldscr::ldsc_h2(munged_sumstats = ldsc_input, ancestry = "AFR") fit <- BRIERs.selection(   fit, criteria = "GIC",    TN = max(dat$target.ss$NMISS),    h2 = max(0, h2_res$h2_observed) ) # Best eta: 6.158  # Best lambda: 0 out <- plot.eta(fit)

text: preprocessS() prepares SNP-level summary-statistic inputs BRIERs() target-cohort individual-level genotypes available. function harmonizes target summary statistics, target LD reference-panel SNP information, optional external model coefficients, aligns inputs SNP order allele orientation target LD reference panel.

title: Preprocess summary statistics for BRIERs genotype-based modeling — preprocessS
code: preprocessS(   target.ss,   target.ind = c("gwas", "corr"),   target.ld,   external.ss = NULL,   target.ss.cols = c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "REF", alt = "ALT", p = "pval", n =     "n", sgn = "sgn", beta = "beta", corr = "corr"),   target.ld.cols = c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "REF", alt = "ALT"),   external.ss.cols = c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "REF", alt = "ALT"),   external.coef.cols = NULL,   drop.ambiguous = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Preprocess summary statistics for BRIERs genotype-based modeling — preprocessS
text: target.ss data.frame target summary statistics. Must contain chromosome, base-pair position, REF, ALT, plus value columns determined target.ind. target.ind One "gwas" "corr". "gwas", target.ss must contain p-value, sample size, either sign beta columns. "corr", correlation column required. target.ld data.frame target LD reference panel SNP info, defining canonical allele orientation. Must contain chromosome, base-pair position, REF, ALT. external.ss Optional data.frame external model coefficients. Must contain chromosome, base-pair position, REF, ALT, plus one coefficient columns. target.ss.cols Named character vector mapping canonical keys (chr, bp, ref, alt, p, n, sgn, beta, corr) column names target.ss. keys relevant target.ind need present. Defaults c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "REF", alt = "ALT", p = "pval", n = "n", sgn = "sgn", beta = "beta", corr = "corr"); override key whose column name differs input. target.ld.cols Named character vector mapping canonical keys (chr, bp, ref, alt) column names target.ld. Defaults c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "REF", alt = "ALT"); override key whose column name differs input. external.ss.cols Named character vector mapping canonical keys (chr, bp, ref, alt) column names external.ss. Defaults c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "REF", alt = "ALT"); override key whose column name differs input. external.coef.cols Character vector column names external.ss hold model coefficients. Required external.ss supplied; function auto-detect. drop.ambiguous Logical. TRUE (default), drops strand-ambiguous SNPs (/T C/G allele pairs) every input matching. verbose Logical. TRUE (default), prints SNP counts filter step.

title: Preprocess summary statistics for BRIERs genotype-based modeling — preprocessS
text: list following elements: target.ss Processed target sumstats data.frame columns varnames, CHR, BP, REF, ALT, corr (LD reference orientation). target.ld Processed target LD reference panel SNP info, subset surviving SNPs, columns varnames, CHR, BP, REF, ALT. target.ld.keep Integer vector indices original target.ld surviving SNPs. Use LD[target.ld.keep, target.ld.keep] subset pre-computed LD matrix. external.ss Processed external coefficients (NULL external input supplied, every supplied external model -zero coefficients alignment), columns varnames, CHR, BP, REF, ALT, coef1, coef2, ... surviving coefficient columns renumbered coef1, ..., coefK input order. external.coef.names Named character vector mapping standardized column names (coef1, coef2, ...) back original column names supplied external.coef.cols, NULL external input survived. summary Named integer vector SNP counts: n.input.tss, n.input.tld, n.input.ess, n.dropped.invalid.chr, n.dropped.ambiguous, n.dropped.multiallelic, n.dropped.unmatched.tss, n.aligned, n.flipped, n.final, n.external.matched, n.external.flipped, n.external.conflict, n.external.zero.

title: Preprocess summary statistics for BRIERs genotype-based modeling — preprocessS
text: argument target.ss data.frame containing target summary statistics. argument target.ld data.frame describing SNPs target LD reference panel, rows ordered match rows columns LD matrix used downstream BRIERs() fitting. optional argument external.ss data.frame containing SNP annotation columns one external coefficient columns, specified external.coef.cols. First, preprocessS() performs quality control harmonization separately target summary statistics, target LD reference panel, external coefficient file. SNP annotation columns standardized CHR, BP, REF, ALT, CHR chromosome, BP base-pair position, REF reference allele, ALT alternative/effect allele. Chromosome labels normalized removing "chr" prefixes, converting PLINK chromosome codes 23, 24, 25, 26 X, Y, XY, MT, converting M MT. SNPs unrecognized chromosome labels removed warning. function also removes sites duplicated CHR:BP entries (treated multi-allelic input duplicates), optionally removes strand-ambiguous SNPs, including /T C/G allele pairs, drop.ambiguous = TRUE. target.ind = "gwas", preprocessS() converts GWAS summary statistics marginal SNP-outcome correlations. setting, target.ss must contain p-values, sample sizes, either effect signs beta coefficients. beta coefficients provided, effect directions inferred sign beta. P-values equal zero replaced smallest observed non-zero p-value computing correlations. p-values zero, function stops error. target.ind = "corr", user must directly provide marginal correlation column. quality control, target LD reference panel used canonical reference SNP order allele orientation. SNP identifiers processed output written CHR:BP:REF:ALT, using LD-reference allele orientation. Target summary-statistic SNPs matched LD reference panel CHR:BP. summary-statistic alleles match LD reference orientation, marginal correlation retained unchanged. summary-statistic REF ALT alleles reversed relative LD reference, marginal correlation sign flipped. SNPs without compatible allele matches target.ss target.ld removed final analysis set. External model coefficients aligned surviving LD-reference SNP set. External SNPs matched CHR:BP. external alleles match LD-reference orientation, external coefficient retained unchanged. external REF ALT alleles reversed relative LD reference, external coefficient sign flipped. alleles conflict, corresponding external coefficient set zero. SNPs present final target/LD SNP set absent external model retained assigned external coefficient zero, whereas SNPs present external model retained output aligned target LD reference panel. External models whose aligned coefficient columns entirely zero removed output. supplied external models removed alignment, function returns external.ss = NULL warning. function errors required annotation, summary-statistic, LD-reference, coefficient column contains missing values. returned target.ld.keep element gives indices SNPs retained original target LD reference panel used subset rows columns precomputed LD matrix calling BRIERs(). returned target.ss contains marginal correlations aligned retained LD-reference SNP order allele orientation. returned external.ss contains external coefficients aligned SNP set. Unlike BRIERi(), BRIERs() require intercept row beta.external.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ out <- preprocessS(   target.ss          = my_gwas,   target.ind         = "gwas",   target.ld          = hapmap3_panel,   external.ss        = prs_coefs,   external.coef.cols = c("PRS_EUR", "PRS_EAS"),   target.ss.cols     = c(     chr  = "Chromosome", bp = "Position",     ref  = "Allele2",    alt = "Allele1",     p    = "PVAL",       n  = "N",     beta = "Effect"   ) )  LD.input <- precomputed_LD[out$target.ld.keep, out$target.ld.keep] beta.ext <- as.matrix(   out$external.ss[, grep("^coef", colnames(out$external.ss)), drop = FALSE] )  fit <- BRIERs(   sumstats = out$target.ss, XtX = LD.input, beta.external = beta.ext, ...) } # }

what: Pseudo-validation score for summary-statistics models — pseudo_validation
title: Pseudo-validation score for summary-statistics models — pseudo_validation
text: Compute pseudo-validation score column coefficient matrix, using sparse LD reference (XtX) marginal correlation vector (r). score defined beta'r / sqrt(beta' XtX beta), used tuning lambda validation data unavailable.

title: Pseudo-validation score for summary-statistics models — pseudo_validation

title: Pseudo-validation score for summary-statistics models — pseudo_validation
text: beta p x L numeric matrix coefficients across L lambda values. XtX p x p sparse LD reference matrix. r numeric vector (length p) marginal correlations genotype outcome.

title: Pseudo-validation score for summary-statistics models — pseudo_validation
text: numeric vector length L containing pseudo-validation score lambda. Non-finite values (e.g. beta zeros) returned NA.

what: Stacking weights for binary responses — stacking_binomial
title: Stacking weights for binary responses — stacking_binomial
text: Compute stacking weights combining multiple external prediction models binomial likelihood. Weights estimated maximising Bernoulli log-likelihood stacked prediction Yw binary response z, using optim. Predictions outside [eps, 1 - eps] penalised large objective value keep optimisation interior unit interval.

Defaults "BFGS". maxit Maximum number iterations optim(). reltol Relative convergence tolerance optim(). eps Numerical tolerance: stacked predictions outside [eps, 1 - eps] penalised big. big Penalty value returned stacked prediction falls outside valid range. larger plausible negative log-likelihood.
